headline: GOLD STUMBLES TO $4,586 as Trump Rejects Iran Peace Deal; Silver Slides Below $74
snippet: Gold fell as much as 2.7% to $4,586/oz on April 28 as President Trump rejected Iran's latest peace proposal delivered via Pakistani mediators, dimming hopes for a quick resolution to the energy supply disruption driving inflation; silver dropped in tandem to below $74.

headline: WORLD BANK WARNS: Iran War to Spark Biggest Energy Price Surge Since 2022 — 24% Commodity Shock
snippet: The World Bank's April 2026 Commodity Markets Outlook projects a 24% surge in global energy prices and a 16% rise in overall commodity prices this year, driven by the Iran war's 10 million barrel-per-day oil supply shock — the largest on record — pushing Brent oil to an average $86/barrel in 2026.

headline: NEW TRIPLET ZONE: Arya Resources Hits 6.05 g/t Au Over 10.85m at Wedge Lake, Saskatchewan
snippet: Arya Resources (TSXV: RBZ) named a new discovery the Triplet Zone after drill hole AR26-16 returned 6.05 g/t Au over 10.85 metres within a broad 35-metre mineralized envelope at Wedge Lake in Saskatchewan's La Ronge Gold Belt — CEO calls it 'a major transformation' for the project.

headline: U.S. MINT Linked to $255M in Colombian Cartel Gold, NYT Investigation Reveals
snippet: A New York Times investigation found that gold from Clan del Golfo-controlled Colombian mines — worth approximately $255 million over the past year — has entered the U.S. Mint's supply chain and been stamped as 'American' on Eagle gold coins, raising oversight and national security questions.

headline: GREENLIGHT METALS Hits 30.7m @ 2.49% CuEq at Bend VMS Deposit in Wisconsin; Zone Extends
snippet: GreenLight Metals (TSX-V: GRL) returned 30.7m of 1.05% Cu and 1.34 g/t Au (2.49% CuEq), including 14.9m of 3.65% CuEq, from the first 2026 assay at the Bend VMS deposit in Wisconsin, confirming down-dip extension of the copper-gold-tellurium system; three more holes are pending assay.

headline: SIXTH STRAIGHT DEFICIT: Silver Tightens as China Curbs Exports and Solar Demand Drains Stockpiles
snippet: Silver is heading into its sixth consecutive annual supply deficit of 67 Moz, with China's new export restrictions on refined silver curbing a key global source since January 2026 while solar and EV industrial demand continues to outpace flat mine production — analysts say materially higher prices are required to rebalance.

headline: 35.3% TREO: Brazilian Rare Earths Reports Ultra High-Grade Results at Monte Alto in Q1
snippet: Brazilian Rare Earths (ASX: BRE) reported intercepts up to 35.3% TREO at Monte Alto in its March 2026 quarterly report, including 27.6m at 19.4% TREO and 23.9m at 17.4% TREO with NdPr grades up to 5.96% — a JORC resource estimate and scoping study are targeted for mid-2026.

headline: $2.8 BILLION DEAL: Is USA Rare Earth's Serra Verde Acquisition Worth the 127M Share Dilution?
snippet: USA Rare Earth (USAR) agreed to acquire Brazil's Serra Verde Group for $300M cash plus 126.8M shares (~$2.8B implied), adding immediate cash flow and heavy rare earth feedstock for its Oklahoma magnet plant while awaiting Round Top's 2028 completion — analysts debate whether dilution outweighs the strategic upside.

headline: U.S. URANIUM DOUBLES UP: UEC Rallies 8.83% as Shirley Basin Joins Burke Hollow in Production
snippet: Uranium Energy Corp (NYSE: UEC) surged 8.83% on April 27 as U.S. uranium production momentum builds, with UEC's Burke Hollow now operating in Texas and Ur-Energy's Shirley Basin ISR project commencing production in Wyoming — uranium spot holds above $85/lb as utilities accelerate long-term contracting.

headline: GOLD EQUITIES STILL CHEAP: One Gold-Silver Miner to Buy in April as Record Margins Go Unpriced
snippet: Motley Fool Canada highlights a gold-silver producer it views as undervalued even with bullion near $4,600/oz, arguing that gold equities still trade at historically modest multiples despite record-high margins — a setup analysts say mirrors previous bull-market re-rating cycles.
